  
**For data extraction on 7/12/2026**

* **Ingestion run one**

|          Table | Records Inserted  |      Duplicates  |             Errors  |
| :---- | :---- | ----- | :---- |
| App | 5 | 0 | 0 |
| Country | 5 | 0 | 0 |
| Raw\_Reviews | 10930 | 0 | 0 |
| Cleaned\_Reviews | 8915 | 0 | 0 |

* **Ingestion run two**

|          Table | Records Inserted  | Duplicates  |             Errors  |
| :---- | :---- | ----- | :---- |
| App | 0 | 5 | 0 |
| Country | 0 | 5 | 0 |
| Raw\_Reviews | 0 | 10930 | 0 |
| Cleaned\_Reviews | 0 | 8915 | 0 |

\-The second ingestion run correctly identified all existing records and skipped duplicate insertions 

* **Quality Flags (records needed to be removed)**

**Sum(condition=True)**

| Duplicate Records  | Missing Review Content | Non-English Review Content | Empty Review After Cleaning |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 0 | 0 | 2015 | 0 |

         **Missing Review Content**  
          \-Reviews with empty or placeholder values (e.g., "", "NA", "N/A",   "Unknown")   
            are flagged because they do not contain meaningful textual information.

**Duplicate Records**  
\-Duplicate reviews are identified using the “review\_id`”`. Only the first occurrence is retained, while subsequent duplicates are flagged to avoid repeated records in downstream analysis.

**Non-English Review Content**  
\-Since the project focuses on English-language text analysis, reviews whose detected language is not English are flagged for removal.

**Empty Review After Cleaning**  
 \-After text preprocessing (such as removing URLs, emojis, punctuation, and other non-textual characters), some reviews may become empty. These records are also flagged because they no longer contain analyzable content.

The quality flags were designed to document why a raw review should be excluded from the **Cleaned\_Reviews dataset** while preserving the original record for traceability.

Instead of deleting records immediately, each raw review is evaluated against a series of predefined quality checks. If a record fails a quality check, a removal reason is recorded in the ‘record\_remove\_reason’ field. This allows every removed record to remain traceable in the **Raw\_Reviews table**.

* **Table relationship**  
    
  1\. All foreign key relationships remained valid after the ingestion process. No orphan records were detected in any of the three relationships.   
    
  2\. The database query results are consistent with the Python ETL Statistics:  
    
  ![][image1]![][image2]  
    
  ![][image3]![][image4]  
    
    
    
    
    
    
  ![][image5]  
  ![][image6]                      ![][image7]

2015 raw review records did not have corresponding records in Cleaned\_Reviews because they were intentionally excluded during the cleaning stage due to non-English content. 

**For data extraction on 7/13/2026**

* **Ingestion run one**

|          Table | Records Inserted  |      Duplicates  |             Errors  |
| :---- | :---- | ----- | :---- |
| App | 0 | 5 | 0 |
| Country | 0 | 5 | 0 |
| Raw\_Reviews | 1 | 10931 | 0 |
| Cleaned\_Reviews | 4 | 8919 | 0 |

    

*    **Quality Flags** 

**Sum(condition=True)**

| Duplicate Records  | Missing Review Content | Non-English Review Content | Empty Review After Cleaning |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| 0 | 1 | 1997 | 0 |

**![][image8]**

This time, the pipeline seemed to correctly skip duplicate records, as shown by the duplicate counts above. However, when new RSS data became available, I noticed that Raw\_Reviews inserted one new record, while Cleaned\_Reviews inserted four new records instead of one**.** 

I checked the Python table records and the database records, and I found that for the same raw\_review\_pk, there are two different records in the Python pipeline and the database, respectively.

I think this happens because raw\_review\_pk is generated in Python based on the row order of the extracted dataset. However, the App Store RSS feed is dynamic, and the order of reviews can change between ingestion runs; the same row number may correspond to a different review in subsequent runs. 

However, if raw\_review\_pk is generated automatically by SQL, I cannot populate the raw\_review\_pk foreign key in Cleaned\_Reviews because those values are not available during the ETL process. 

I would appreciate any design advice for addressing this issue.
